Icacorea, also known as the Guanabani, is a plant genus of flowering plants in the family Icacinaceae. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, helping to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues.

The bioactive constituents of Icacorea include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, such as icacin, icacinine, and polyphenols, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

Common herbal preparations made from Icacorea include teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as extracts and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of these preparations.
